name: Mitosis
description: A living cell colony rendered by Gray–Scott reaction–diffusion: abstract glowing cells on dark that visibly divide (mitosis) and merge in time with the music. Drum/percussive onsets trigger visible bursts of cell division on the beat (the colony buds new cells); sustained energy sets the division rate and density, shifting the colony between sparse and teeming; cells divide and merge across the song. The discrete, localized, on-beat division reaction-diffusion delivers natively is the tight event-sync the physarum trail substrate (Filigree) could not carry. Two-texture (A,B) ping-pong compute, no new render primitive. Graduated from the throwaway reaction-diffusion sketch (MITOSIS.0).
author: Matt
